hypothesis: Incorporating electron beam dose rate as a dynamic conditioning variable during SAM-EM fine-tuning—by generating synthetic training data that explicitly pairs spatiotemporally varying dose rate maps with their corresponding image contrast degradation and beam-induced particle motion artifacts—will enable the model to disentangle beam-driven dynamics from intrinsic nanoparticle diffusion, thereby allowing real-time classification of particle trajectories as thermally driven, beam-driven, or mixed, which would be falsifiable by comparing diffusion coefficients extracted by the conditioned model against those measured under systematically varied dose rates in controlled LPTEM experiments where beam-induced effects are independently quantified.
